warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Method–provenance summary
Property: condensed Fukui f+ reactivity index of the most electrophilic atom in nitrobenzene (atom 4, C)
Level of theory: DFT tier `standard`  (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF
Geometry provenance: user-supplied xyz (…/nitrobenzene.xyz); single-point charges on that fixed geometry (no re-optimization)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (cation/anion runs were at +1, 2 and –1, 2 to obtain N±1 states)
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF 2.x backend for DFT
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for neutral, cation and anion all converged; no engine errors.  The integrity checker reports that Σf+ = Σf– = 1.0000 (expected ≈ 1), flagged only as warnings (see below).

Headline result
Most electrophilic site (highest f+): atom 4 (C);  f+ = 0.49641784

Reproducibility
CLI invocation recorded in result JSON:
  /Users/evane/.../cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard nitrobenzene.xyz --out …/live.json
The engine’s stdout/stderr was streamed live to the default fukui_<timestamp>.out log in the working directory; see that file for SCF and charge-analysis details.

Warnings echoed verbatim
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
